records.ety: [LL. funeralia, prop. neut. pl. of funeralis of a funeral, fr. L. funus, funeris, funeral: cf. F. funrailles.]

defs: The solemn rites used in the disposition of a dead human body, whether such disposition be by interment, burning, or otherwise; esp., the ceremony or solemnization of interment; obsequies; burial; -- formerly used in the plural.
quotes: King James his funerals were performed very solemnly in the collegiate church at Westminster.

defs: The procession attending the burial of the dead; the show and accompaniments of an interment.
A funeral sermon; -- usually in the plural.
quotes: Mr. Giles Lawrence preached his funerals.

defs: Pertaining to a funeral; used at the interment of the dead; as, funeral rites, honors, or ceremonies.
